title: Elwynn Forest
short_description: a rolling green forest under Stormwind's banner, south of the city's gates
content: A rolling green forest under Stormwind's protection, southwest of the great city's gates. Goldshire's inn is a waystation for travelers, guards, and farmhands; Northshire Abbey to the north trains the Light's clergy. The Defias have been quieter of late, but bandits and the kobold-ridden mineshafts still trouble the roads. The roads remember the First War, when the orc horde poured through Elwynn on its way to Stormwind Keep; the older farmers still point out fields where the broken siege-engines lay.

title: Durotar
short_description: a harsh red canyon land claimed by the Horde, east of Orgrimmar's iron walls
content: A harsh red land of canyon and scrub, claimed by the Horde and watched from the iron spires of Orgrimmar. The Valley of Trials still tests young orcs before they ride for the front. Razor Hill garrisons the road north; the Echo Isles to the south have been restored to the Darkspear after long exile. Boars, scorpids, quilboar, and the Burning Blade cult give the land's hardier youth no shortage of foes to prove themselves against.

title: Teldrassil
short_description: a vast World Tree of the kaldorei rising from the sea, holding Darnassus in its boughs
content: The World Tree of the kaldorei, raised by Fandral Staghelm against Cenarius's counsel — a vast branching city holding Darnassus in its boughs. Shadowglen at the roots is where the night elves' young initiates first wake under the moon. Dolanaar watches the southern road. The corrupted furbolgs of Ban'ethil and the lurking spiders of the eastern groves stain the Tree's protection, and not all druids of the Cenarion Circle approve of Teldrassil's existence at all.

title: Mulgore
short_description: wide grassland homeland of the Bloodhoof tauren, ringed by bluffs in central Kalimdor
content: The wide grassland the Bloodhoof claim as their homeland, ringed by bluffs and mesas in central Kalimdor. Bloodhoof Village watches the herd-paths; Thunder Bluff rises in the north on four great mesas joined by rope bridges and elevators. Cairne and his elders teach the young to honor the elements and the spirits of the hunt. The harpies of the Wyverntail and the centaur of the Gelkis and Magram still raid the herds, but the Tauren ride them down with relish.

title: Dun Morogh
short_description: snowbound dwarven highlands of Khaz Modan, where Ironforge burns at the mountain's heart
content: The snowbound highlands of the dwarves, where Ironforge burns hot at the mountain's heart. Anvilmar in the south is where the youngest Bronzebeards take their first hammer; Kharanos serves a fine ale to travelers crossing the pass. Coldridge Valley shelters its own. The Frostmane trolls still raid from their hidden caves, and the Dark Iron tunneling up from Shadowforge City beneath the Searing Gorge has never truly stopped.

title: Tirisfal Glades
short_description: grey sickly woodland north of the Plaguelands, cradle of the Forsaken and the Dark Lady's seat
content: Sickly grey woodland north of the Plaguelands, the cradle of the Forsaken. Deathknell is where the newly raised come back to themselves and pledge to the Dark Lady's cause. Brill stands further east, and beneath Lordaeron's ruined capital sprawls the Undercity itself — Sylvanas's seat, and the apothecaries' workshops. The Scarlet Crusade still festers in Hearthglen and the Monastery and hate the Forsaken with a zealot's purity. The Scourge are quieter here than once, but the dead have not forgotten Lordaeron.

title: Azuremyst Isle
short_description: a wooded island west of Kalimdor where the draenei ship Exodar crashed and still smolders
content: A wooded island west of Kalimdor, where the crystalline dimensional ship Exodar fell from the sky and gouged a scar through the trees. The Crash Site still smokes; Azure Watch's draenei refugees salvage what they can from the wreckage. The crystals that fell with the ship corrupted the wildlife — owlkin, moths, and stags warped by raw arcane energy. To the south the satyr of Ammen Vale and the naga of the western coast remain steady threats. The draenei here are weary, but unbroken.

title: Eversong Woods
short_description: the golden autumn forest of Quel'Thalas, rebuilt around Silvermoon after the Scourge's ruin
content: The golden autumn forest of Quel'Thalas, restored at terrible cost after the Scourge's invasion. Silvermoon City glitters at its heart, every spire rebuilt; Falconwing Square trains the new initiates of the blood. Sunstrider Isle to the east tests the youngest sin'dorei before they are deemed sin'dorei in truth. The wraiths and starving mana-creatures of Lake Elrendar are reminders that Arthas's blight is not so easily scoured. The blood elves do not forget the price they paid here.

title: Borean Tundra
short_description: wind-stripped tundra on Northrend's western coast, contested by both Alliance and Horde beachheads
content: The western coast of Northrend — wind-stripped tundra, steaming geothermal vents, and the ruined halls of the nerubian dead. Valiance Keep flies the lion of Stormwind on the southern shore; Warsong Hold the wolf of Orgrimmar to its east. The Riplash Ruins still smoke from the long war between the nerubians and the blue dragonflight, whose Coldarra holds Malygos's frozen seat. The Scourge stir from beneath the ice. Newcomers learn the cold the hard way, or not at all.

title: Howling Fjord
short_description: dark clifftop fjords on Northrend's eastern coast, haunted by vrykul raiders from Utgarde Keep
content: The eastern coast of Northrend — dark cliffs, deep fjords, and the longhouses of the vrykul who serve the Lich King as half-giant raiders. Valgarde harbors the Alliance landing; Camp Winterhoof shelters the Horde's. Utgarde Keep, the vrykul fortress on the heights, has not fallen — and below it the Dragonflayer clan still raise their dead to fight again. The bay-eels and proto-dragons of the coast care nothing for either banner.

title: Westfall
short_description: Stormwind's breadbasket gone fallow, where abandoned farms and the Defias Brotherhood trouble the southern road
content: Once the granary that fed Stormwind, Westfall has fallen to ruin and dust, its farmsteads empty and its fields choked with weeds. The displaced laborers who built the city and were never paid turned outlaw, and now the masked Defias Brotherhood holds the broken country as its own. From Sentinel Hill the People's Militia keeps a thin watch over the roads, harried by harvest-golems gone wild and the gnolls of the Dagger Hills. Along the coast, a hidden cove shelters the Brotherhood's greatest secret, a thing of timber and iron that should not exist.

title: Stormwind City
short_description: the Alliance capital of marble towers and canals, where unrest stirs in the prison beneath the streets
content: Stormwind rises in white marble and blue banners, the proudest of the human kingdoms reborn from the ashes of the First War. Its districts ring out with the work of trade, the forge, the cathedral of the Light, and the magus' quarter, while King Varian Wrynn holds court in the keep above the harbor. Beneath the Canal District lies the city's prison, and word has spread that the Defias and cutthroats locked within have overpowered their jailers and seized the lower halls. The guard musters at the gates, for a riot in the dark below the streets is no small thing in the heart of the Alliance.

title: Silverpine Forest
short_description: a misty Forsaken-held forest between Tirisfal and Hillsbrad, shadowed by the Greymane Wall
content: Silverpine is a forest of perpetual mist and grey light, claimed by the Forsaken as they press south from Tirisfal toward Hillsbrad. The ruined village of Pyrewood lies cursed, its folk turning to beasts at nightfall, and the great Greymane Wall seals the southwestern road against whatever Gilneas became behind it. The Dark Lady's deathstalkers watch the highways while feral worgen prowl the deeper woods. Above a lonely crag stands a brooding keep where a mad noble's experiments left only ghosts and worgen behind.

title: Blackrock Mountain
short_description: the great volcano between Searing Gorge and the Burning Steppes, riddled with the warrens of dwarf, orc, and dragon
content: Where Searing Gorge meets the Burning Steppes, a single black volcano hollowed by ages of digging holds more war than any mountain should. The Dark Iron dwarves carved their imperial city into its depths and woke Ragnaros in its molten heart; the remnants of the old Horde hold the high spires under Rend Blackhand; and coiled above them all, the black dragon Nefarian breeds his monstrous brood. Alliance and Horde both send their boldest into the mountain, and most of what goes in stays.

title: Western Plaguelands
short_description: blighted farmland slowly reclaimed by the Argent Dawn, scarred by Andorhal and the necromancers' school
content: The Western Plaguelands were once the fertile fields of Lordaeron, and now the Argent Dawn fights field by field to cleanse the blight that the Scourge sowed here. Andorhal lies contested between the living and the dead, while the Scarlet Crusade clings to Hearthglen and burns anything it deems tainted. On the island of Caer Darrow, across a haunted causeway, a school of necromancy festers in the cellars of a drowned manor, teaching the dark arts to those who would raise the dead. The Sons of Lothar and the cenarion druids labor to call life back to the poisoned earth.

title: Eastern Plaguelands
short_description: the deepest blight of Lordaeron, where ruined Stratholme and the Scourge's strongholds still fester
content: Here the blight runs deepest, a red and rotting land where the very sky hangs sick over the bones of Lordaeron. The ruined city of Stratholme, where Arthas first turned to darkness, remains divided between the Scourge undead and the fanatic Scarlet Crusade who hold the bastion of Tyr's Hand. The Argent Dawn keeps a defiant outpost at Light's Hope Chapel, a single candle against the dark. Plaguebats, abominations, and the restless dead roam the ash-grey hills, and the four towers of the old kingdom now serve the Scourge as beacons of corruption.

title: Swamp of Sorrows
short_description: a fetid mire east of the Blasted Lands, hiding the sunken temple of a dragon and a troll god's cult
content: The Swamp of Sorrows is a drowned and fetid country east of the Blasted Lands, its air thick with rot and the croak of unseen things. The Stonard outpost gives the Horde a foothold among the muck, while the broken Stonard road runs toward the dark fortress of the Dark Portal beyond. At the swamp's heart, in the murky depths of a flooded lake, lies a sunken temple where the green dragon Eranikus lies trapped and the Atal'ai cultists of the blood-god still chant for the return of their fallen master. Crocolisks, slimes, and the cult's fanatics make the mire no place for the careless.

title: Stranglethorn Vale
short_description: a vast steaming jungle of trolls and pirates, where the ruins of the Gurubashi empire choke beneath the vines
content: Stranglethorn is a vast and steaming jungle at the southern reach of the Eastern Kingdoms, alive with panthers, raptors, and the great tigers the trolls revere. The ruins of the old Gurubashi empire lie scattered beneath the canopy, half-swallowed by root and vine, while the goblins of Booty Bay broker neutral trade on the southern coast and bloodsail pirates raid the waters. Deep in the vines stands the troll city where the priests of the blood-god Hakkar work to call their devourer back into the world. Adventurers come for the hunt, the gold, and the old treasures the jungle has not yet claimed.

title: Badlands
short_description: a cracked red canyon land of Dark Iron diggers and Stonevault troggs over a buried titan vault
content: The Badlands are a harsh expanse of cracked red stone and dust, a canyon country south of Loch Modan where little grows and the wind never rests. The Dark Iron dwarves dig here for something buried deep, and the Stonevault troggs swarm up from below to fight them for it. At Kargath the Horde keeps a grim outpost, while dragonkin and rock elementals stalk the broken hills. Beneath it all lies a sealed vault of the titans, a place of stone guardians and old secrets that both the dwarves and darker powers would give much to unseal.

title: Deadwind Pass
short_description: a dead, fog-choked canyon between Duskwood and the Swamp, brooded over by Medivh's silent tower
content: Deadwind Pass is a narrow, fog-choked canyon linking Duskwood to the Swamp of Sorrows, and nothing living thrives there. Ogres of the Deadwind clan haunt its ruined camps, and ravens wheel over the dead trees as though waiting. Above it all looms a great dark tower, the abandoned seat of the last Guardian Medivh, where the Burning Legion once whispered through a corrupted mind and opened the way for the orcish horde. The tower stands silent now, but the curse upon it has never lifted, and ghostly echoes of revelry still drift down from its halls.
